paper.title: Towards surveillance video search by natural language query
paper.from_query: semantic video retrieval AND action recognition AND natural language query
paper.abstract: Spatial language video retrieval is an important real-world problem that is also a natural test bed for evaluating semantic structures for natural language descriptions of motion on naturalistic data. This paper describes first steps towards a system that grounds the meaning of spatial prepositions in geometric features. This system can be used to search a corpus of surveillance video for clips that match spatial language queries such as "along the hallway" and "across the kitchen." We present experiments characterizing the performance of models for the prepositions "across" and "along," and present a methodology for modeling other spatial prepositions.

llm_screening.Key technologies / methods: grounding spatial prepositions in geometric features, modeling spatial prepositions
llm_screening.Notes: Proposes a method to ground spatial prepositions in geometric features for video search
llm_screening.Reason of relevance: Addresses task relevant area and supports natural language queries
Top evidence: "Towards surveillance video search by natural language query", "match spatial language queries"
